name: Erdős–Straus Theorem

phase_0.boundary_condition: The closed set of all valid Egyptian fraction representations of 4/n for any positive integer n ≥ 2

VINIT.justification: The void state before any Egyptian fraction decomposition is attempted; the raw input awaiting transformation

TANCH.chosen_element: the theorem's universal claim: ∀n ≥ 2, ∃ unit fractions summing to 4/n
TANCH.justification: The hermetic boundary that contains and validates all decompositions; the constitutional anchor of the entire system

AFWD.chosen_element: greedy algorithm selection: choose largest unit fraction ≤ 4/n
AFWD.justification: Forward morphism toward solution; the directed transformation that advances decomposition

AREV.justification: Reverse/descent transformation; the contravariant step that simplifies the problem by extracting a unit fraction

CLINK.chosen_element: sequential composition of greedy steps: repeat (select unit fraction, subtract, recurse)
CLINK.justification: Chaining of morphisms; each decomposition step links to the next via remainder

IMSCRIB.justification: Self-reference; the element recognizes itself as the goal state when decomposition terminates

FSPLIT.chosen_element: case split on n modulo 4: n ≡ 0, 1, 2, 3 (mod 4)
FSPLIT.justification: Branches 4/n into four distinct congruence classes; each class has its own decomposition strategy

FFUSE.chosen_element: reconstitution: all four congruence cases yield valid Egyptian fraction decompositions that sum to 4/n
FFUSE.justification: Fuses the four branches back into a single unified result; FFUSE(FSPLIT(4/n)) = 4/n (the original rational is recovered as a sum of unit fractions)

EVALT.chosen_element: decomposition succeeds: remainder becomes 0 or all terms are unit fractions
EVALT.justification: Affirmative branch; the positive evaluation that the algorithm has found a valid representation

EVALF.chosen_element: decomposition fails: remainder is negative or algorithm cannot proceed
EVALF.justification: Negative branch; the failure state indicating the current path does not yield a valid unit fraction

ENGAGR.chosen_element: the paradice of multiple valid decompositions: 4/n admits more than one Egyptian fraction representation
ENGAGR.justification: Both simultaneously held without resolution; the theorem guarantees existence but does not mandate uniqueness—multiple valid decompositions coexist

IFIX.chosen_element: permanent record: each unit fraction 1/x selected is appended to the final decomposition list
IFIX.justification: Append-only, irreversible fixation; once a unit fraction is chosen and recorded, it cannot be undone or modified

phase_2.fuse_element: reconstitution of all four branches into a single Egyptian fraction sum
phase_2.fuse_result: 4/n expressed as a sum of unit fractions (the original rational is recovered)

phase_3.void_description: 00 — the uninitialized state before 4/n is given; no decomposition has begun
phase_3.true_description: 01 — a valid Egyptian fraction decomposition has been found; all terms are unit fractions and sum to 4/n
phase_3.false_description: 10 — the current decomposition path has failed; remainder is negative or algorithm cannot proceed
phase_3.both_description: 11 — the paradice state: multiple distinct valid Egyptian fraction representations of 4/n coexist simultaneously, each valid, none privileged

steps.domain_action: uninitialized rational 4/n awaits decomposition
the theorem's universal boundary is established—∀n ≥ 2, a decomposition exists
branch on n modulo 4 into four congruence classes
on the n ≡ 0 (mod 4) branch, select the largest unit fraction 1/(n/4)
n ≡ 0 case succeeds immediately; 4/n = 1/(n/4) is a unit fraction
record 1/(n/4) as a permanent term in the decomposition
FFUSE.T: the n ≡ 0 branch reconstitutes to 4/n
on the n ≡ 1 (mod 4) branch, compute remainder after selecting 1/⌈n/4⌉
chain the remainder (4⌈n/4⌉ − n)/(n⌈n/4⌉) back into the algorithm
if remainder is a unit fraction, accept it
record the unit fraction as a permanent term
FFUSE.T: the n ≡ 1 branch reconstitutes to 4/n
on the n ≡ 2 (mod 4) branch, compute remainder after selecting 1/⌈n/4⌉
chain the remainder back into the algorithm
if remainder is a unit fraction, accept it
record the unit fraction as a permanent term
FFUSE.T: the n ≡ 2 branch reconstitutes to 4/n
on the n ≡ 3 (mod 4) branch, compute remainder after selecting 1/⌈n/4⌉
chain the remainder back into the algorithm
if remainder is a unit fraction, accept it
record the unit fraction as a permanent term
FFUSE.T: the n ≡ 3 branch reconstitutes to 4/n
all four branches fuse back into a single unified result—4/n is now expressed as a sum of unit fractions
the decomposition recognizes itself as complete; 4/n = 4/n (identity confirmed)
acknowledge that multiple valid decompositions may exist simultaneously for the same 4/n
the final decomposition is permanently recorded and cannot be altered
the theorem's boundary is satisfied; the decomposition is valid and complete

phase_5.compiler_frontend: the greedy algorithm and modular arithmetic rules that translate the rational 4/n into a sequence of unit fraction selections
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: remainder passing between successive decomposition steps; each step communicates its remainder to the next via the CLINK composition
phase_5.memory_mechanism: the permanent list of selected unit fractions, stored via IFIX; the current remainder state, held in registers during computation
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: the modular case analysis (n mod 4) determines which decomposition strategy is applied; greedy selection determines the order of unit fraction extraction
phase_5.alfs_store: the Erdős–Straus theorem itself: the canonical reference that guarantees existence of a decomposition for all n ≥ 2

phase_6.cycle_cost: one unit fraction selection per cycle; one IFIX record per term added to the decomposition
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: the rational 4/n in undecomposed form; no unit fractions selected yet
phase_6.post_cycle_state: the rational 4/n expressed as a sum of unit fractions; all terms permanently recorded
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the decomposition is a reversible transformation: the sum of unit fractions can be recombined to recover 4/n, and the theorem guarantees that the process always terminates with a valid representation

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → TANCH → FSPLIT → AFWD → EVALT → IFIX → FFUSE → AREV → CLINK → EVALT → IFIX → FFUSE → AREV → CLINK → EVALT → IFIX → FFUSE → AREV → CLINK → EVALT → IFIX → FFUSE → FFUSE → IMSCRIB → ENGAGR → IFIX → TANCH
-- Class: Erdős–Straus Theorem
-- Fingerprint: sig=(11,6,5,5)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=False | period=27
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(2, 6)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑡  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑼 → 𐑚  | terminal object — connectivity boundary
--   [2] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [3] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑚 → 𐑙  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [4] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [5] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑚 → 𐑙  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [6] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑗  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [7] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑙 → 𐑱  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [8] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑗 → ⊙  | composition — regime coherence
--   [9] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑱 → 𐑭  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [10] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               ⊙ → 𐑙  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [11] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑭 → 𐑗  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [12] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑙 → 𐑱  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [13] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑗 → ⊙  | composition — regime coherence
--   [14] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑱 → 𐑭  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [15] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               ⊙ → 𐑙  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [16] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑭 → 𐑗  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [17] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑙 → 𐑱  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [18] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑗 → ⊙  | composition — regime coherence
--   [19] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑱 → 𐑭  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [20] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               ⊙ → 𐑙  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [21] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑭 → 𐑙  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [22] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑠  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [23] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑙 → 𐑳  | identity — self-imscription
--   [24] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑠 → 𐑭  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [25] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑳 → 𐑡  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [26] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑭 → 𐑼  | terminal object — connectivity boundary

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def erd_s_straus_theorem_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑡)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (uninitialized rational 4/n awaits decomposition)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑼 𐑚)  -- [1] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (the theorem's universal boundary is established—∀n ≥ 2, a decomposition exists)
  -- FSPLIT [2] (gran := 𐑚) (branch on n modulo 4 into four congruence classes) / FFUSE [6] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (3 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑾 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [3] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (on the n ≡ 0 (mod 4) branch, select the largest unit fraction 1/(n/4))
      .seq
        (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [4] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (n ≡ 0 case succeeds immediately; 4/n = 1/(n/4) is a unit fraction)
        (.arrow 𐑭 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [5] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (record 1/(n/4) as a permanent term in the decomposition)
      -- F-branch (0 nodes)
      (.refl 𐑙))  -- F-branch: empty arc (direct to FFUSE.F)
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [6]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑗)  -- [6] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (FFUSE.T: the n ≡ 0 branch reconstitutes to 4/n)
  (.arrow 𐑗 𐑙 𐑱)  -- [7] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (on the n ≡ 1 (mod 4) branch, compute remainder after selecting 1/⌈n/4⌉)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑗 ⊙)  -- [8] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (chain the remainder (4⌈n/4⌉ − n)/(n⌈n/4⌉) back into the algorithm)
  (.arrow ⊙ 𐑱 𐑭)  -- [9] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (if remainder is a unit fraction, accept it)
  (.arrow 𐑭 ⊙ 𐑙)  -- [10] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (record the unit fraction as a permanent term)
  (.arrow 𐑙 𐑭 𐑗)  -- [11] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 | fuse μ — assembly mode (FFUSE.T: the n ≡ 1 branch reconstitutes to 4/n)
  (.arrow 𐑗 𐑙 𐑱)  -- [12] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (on the n ≡ 2 (mod 4) branch, compute remainder after selecting 1/⌈n/4⌉)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑗 ⊙)  -- [13] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (chain the remainder back into the algorithm)
  (.arrow ⊙ 𐑱 𐑭)  -- [14] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (if remainder is a unit fraction, accept it)
  (.arrow 𐑭 ⊙ 𐑙)  -- [15] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (record the unit fraction as a permanent term)
  (.arrow 𐑙 𐑭 𐑗)  -- [16] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 | fuse μ — assembly mode (FFUSE.T: the n ≡ 2 branch reconstitutes to 4/n)
  (.arrow 𐑗 𐑙 𐑱)  -- [17] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (on the n ≡ 3 (mod 4) branch, compute remainder after selecting 1/⌈n/4⌉)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑗 ⊙)  -- [18] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (chain the remainder back into the algorithm)
  (.arrow ⊙ 𐑱 𐑭)  -- [19] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (if remainder is a unit fraction, accept it)
  (.arrow 𐑭 ⊙ 𐑙)  -- [20] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (record the unit fraction as a permanent term)
  (.arrow 𐑙 𐑭 𐑙)  -- [21] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 | fuse μ — assembly mode (FFUSE.T: the n ≡ 3 branch reconstitutes to 4/n)
  (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑠)  -- [22] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 | fuse μ — assembly mode (all four branches fuse back into a single unified result—4/n is now expressed...)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑙 𐑳)  -- [23] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (the decomposition recognizes itself as complete; 4/n = 4/n (identity confirmed))
  (.arrow 𐑳 𐑠 𐑭)  -- [24] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (acknowledge that multiple valid decompositions may exist simultaneously for t...)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑳 𐑡)  -- [25] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (the final decomposition is permanently recorded and cannot be altered)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑭 𐑼)  -- [26] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (the theorem's boundary is satisfied; the decomposition is valid and complete)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def erd_s_straus_theorem_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (erd_s_straus_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem erd_s_straus_theorem_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

-- Frobenius (split → fuse): μ∘δ = id on .prod branch
-- Proof: apply igFrobAlg_self_fusion; exact mu_delta_A_id
-- (requires mu_delta_A_id from IGFunctor library)

end Imscribing
